hierarchy.title: भारतीय įरज़वर् बैंक RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
hierarchy.text:
भारतीय įरज़वर् बैंक RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
RBI/DOR/2025-26/245 DOR.ORG.REC.No.164/21-04-158/2025-26


November 28, 2025
Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks - Managing Risks in Outsourcing)
Directions, 2025

children.title: Table of Contents
children.text: Table of Contents

hierarchy.title: CHAPTER III - OUTSOURCING OF INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (IT) SERVICES ............. 9
hierarchy.text: CHAPTER III - OUTSOURCING OF INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (IT) SERVICES ............. 9

children.title: <image>
F. Redressal of Grievances related to Outsourced Services ................................................ 28
children.text: F. Redressal of Grievances related to Outsourced Services ................................................ 28

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
In exercise of the powers conferred by Section 35A of the Banking Regulation Act,
1949, and all other provisions / laws enabling the Reserve Bank of India ('RBI') in this
regard, RBI being satisfied that it is necessary and expedient in the public interest so to
do, hereby, issues the Directions hereinafter specified.

children.title: A. Short Title and Commencement
children.text:
A. Short Title and Commencement
1.
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Local Area Banks -
Managing Risks in Outsourcing) Directions, 2025.
2.
These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
Provided that a bank's existing Information Technology (IT) outsourcing
agreements regardless of whether they are due for renewal on or after the
effective date of these Directions shall comply with the provisions of these
Directions either at the time of renewal or by April 10, 2026, whichever is earlier.
However, the bank's new IT outsourcing agreements that come into force on or
after the effective date of these Directions, shall comply with the provisions of
these Directions from the date of agreement itself.
Provided further that nothing in the preceding proviso shall be construed as
permitting non-compliance with any other extant regulatory instructions or statutory
requirements applicable to such arrangements.

children.title: B. Applicability and Scope
children.text:
B. Applicability and Scope
3.
These Directions shall be applicable to Local Area Banks (hereinafter collectively
referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank').
4.
These Directions shall apply to a bank's material outsourcing of IT services, as
defined in paragraph 6(3) of these Directions. In this context, 'Outsourcing of IT
Services' shall include outsourcing of the following services:

children.title: (i) IT infrastructure management, maintenance, and support (hardware,
children.text:
(i) IT infrastructure management, maintenance, and support (hardware,
software or firmware);

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (ii) network and security solutions, and maintenance (hardware, software or
children.text:
(ii) network and security solutions, and maintenance (hardware, software or
firmware);

children.title: (iii) application development, maintenance, and testing by Application Service
children.text:
(iii) application development, maintenance, and testing by Application Service
Providers (ASPs) including ATM Switch ASPs;

children.title: (iv) services and operations related to data centres;
children.text: (iv) services and operations related to data centres;

children.title: (v) cloud computing services;
children.text: (v) cloud computing services;

children.title: (vi) managed security services; and
children.text: (vi) managed security services; and

children.title: (vii) management of IT infrastructure and technology services associated with
children.text:
(vii) management of IT infrastructure and technology services associated with
payment system ecosystem.
5.
These Directions shall not apply to the following services:

children.title: (i) corporate internet banking services obtained by a bank as a corporate
children.text:
(i) corporate internet banking services obtained by a bank as a corporate
customer or sub-member of another Regulated Entity (RE);
Provided that for the purpose of these Directions, the following indicative (but
not exhaustive) list of entities shall be considered as REs - Commercial
Banks; other LABs; Small Finance Banks; Payments Banks; Regional Rural
Banks; Non-Banking Financial Companies in Base Layer (NBFC - BL),
Middle Layer (NBFC - ML), and Upper Layer (NBFC - UL); All India Financial
Institutions; Credit Information Companies; Urban Co-operative Banks; Rural
Co-operative Banks; and, Payment System Operators.
For the purpose of these Directions, 'Commercial Banks' means banking
companies (other than Small Finance Banks, Local Area Banks, Payments
Banks and Regional Rural Banks), corresponding new banks, and the State
Bank of India as defined respectively under clauses (c), (da), and (nc) of
section 5 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.

children.title: (ii) external audit services such as Vulnerability Assessment (VA) / Penetration
children.text:
(ii) external audit services such as Vulnerability Assessment (VA) / Penetration
Testing (PT), Information Systems Audit, and security review;

children.title: (iii) SMS gateways (including bulk SMS service providers);
children.text: (iii) SMS gateways (including bulk SMS service providers);

children.title: (iv) procurement of IT hardware or appliances;
children.text: (iv) procurement of IT hardware or appliances;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (v) acquisition of IT software, product, or application (e.g., Core Banking Solution
children.text:
(v) acquisition of IT software, product, or application (e.g., Core Banking Solution
(CBS), database, and security solutions) on a licence or subscription basis,
and any enhancements made to such licensed third-party applications by the
vendor (as upgrades) or on specific change request made by a bank;

children.title: (vi) any maintenance service (including security patches, bug fixes) for IT
children.text:
(vi) any maintenance service (including security patches, bug fixes) for IT
infrastructure or licensed products, provided by the Original Equipment
Manufacturer (OEM) themselves, in order to ensure continued usage of the
same by the bank;

children.title: (vii) applications provided by financial sector regulators or institutions such as
(viii) platforms provided by entities such as Reuters, Bloomberg, and Society for
children.text:
(viii) platforms provided by entities such as Reuters, Bloomberg, and Society for
Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication (SWIFT);

children.title: (ix) any other off-the-shelf products (e.g., anti-virus software, and email solutions)
(x) services obtained by a bank as a sub-member of a Centralised Payment
children.text:
(x) services obtained by a bank as a sub-member of a Centralised Payment
System (CPS) from another RE;

children.title: (xi) Business Correspondent (BC) services, payroll processing, and statement
children.text:
(xi) Business Correspondent (BC) services, payroll processing, and statement
printing.

children.title: C. Definitions
children.text:
C. Definitions
6.
In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'Group' shall be as defined in the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial
Banks - Concentration Risk Management) Directions, 2025, as amended
from time to time, for the purpose of intragroup transactions and
exposures.
(2) 'IT services' means IT services / IT enabled services / IT activities.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(3) 'Material Outsourcing of IT Services' are those which:

children.title: (i) if disrupted or compromised shall have the potential to significantly
children.text:
(i) if disrupted or compromised shall have the potential to significantly
impact the bank's business operations; or

children.title: (ii) may have material impact on the bank's customers in the event of
children.text:
(ii) may have material impact on the bank's customers in the event of
any unauthorised access, loss or theft of customer information.
(4) 'Outsourcing' means use of a third party (either an affiliated entity within a
corporate group or an entity that is external to the corporate group) by a
bank to perform activities on a continuing basis that would normally be
undertaken by the bank itself, now or in the future. 'Continuing basis' shall
include agreements for a limited period.
(5) 'Service Provider' means provider of IT services including entities related
to the bank or those which belong to the same group or conglomerate to
which the bank belongs.
Provided that for the purpose of these Directions, the following indicative
(but not exhaustive) list of vendors and entities shall not be considered as
'Service Providers' defined above:

children.title: (i) vendors providing business services using IT e.g., BCs);
children.text: (i) vendors providing business services using IT e.g., BCs);

children.title: (ii) Payment System Operators (PSOs) authorised by RBI under the
(iii) partnership based FinTech firms such as those providing co-
children.text:
(iii) partnership based FinTech firms such as those providing co-
branded applications, products, and services;

children.title: (iv) FinTech firms providing services for data retrieval, data validation
children.text:
(iv) FinTech firms providing services for data retrieval, data validation
and verification such as, bank statement analysis, GST returns
analysis, fetching of vehicle information, digital document execution,
data entry and call centre services;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (v) telecom service providers from whom leased lines or other similar
(vi) security or audit consultants appointed for certification, audit or VA /
children.text:
(vi) security or audit consultants appointed for certification, audit or VA /
PT related to IT infrastructure, IT services, or Information Security
services in their role as independent third-party auditor, consultant
or lead implementer.
(6) 'Sub-contractor' refers to those providing material / significant IT services
to the service provider and is specific to the material / significant IT
services arrangement that the bank has entered into with the service
provider.
7.
All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as have
been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, or the Reserve
Bank of India Act, 1934 or the Information Technology Act, 2000, or the
Companies Act, 2013, and Rules made thereunder, or any statutory modification
or re-enactment thereto, or Glossary of Terms published by RBI, or as used in
commercial parlance, as the case may be.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Chapter II - Role of the Board
hierarchy.text:
Chapter II - Role of the Board
8.
The outsourcing of any IT service by a bank does not diminish its obligations, and
those of its Board and Senior Management, who have the ultimate responsibility
for the outsourced service.

children.title: A. Board-Approved Policy
children.text:
A. Board-Approved Policy
9.
A bank intending to outsource its IT services shall put in place a comprehensive
Board-approved IT outsourcing policy, the coverage of which is indicated in
paragraph 15.

children.title: B. Key Responsibilities
children.text: B. Key Responsibilities

children.title: 10. The Board shall be responsible, inter alia, for:
children.text: 10. The Board shall be responsible, inter alia, for:

children.title: (i) putting in place a framework for approval of outsourcing services depending
children.text:
(i) putting in place a framework for approval of outsourcing services depending
on risks and materiality;

children.title: (ii) approving policies to evaluate the risks and materiality of all existing and
children.text:
(ii) approving policies to evaluate the risks and materiality of all existing and
prospective outsourcing arrangements;

children.title: (iii) setting up suitable administrative framework of Senior Management;
children.text: (iii) setting up suitable administrative framework of Senior Management;

children.title: (iv) ensuring either by itself or through its Committee, that there is no conflict of
children.text:
(iv) ensuring either by itself or through its Committee, that there is no conflict of
interest arising out of third-party engagements, especially when permitting an
exception to the requirement that the service provider of outsourced services,
if not a group company, shall not be owned or controlled by any director, key
managerial personnel, approver of the outsourcing arrangement, or their
relatives as indicated under the proviso to paragraph 12(vi) of these
Directions; and

children.title: (v) reviewing any adverse development mentioned in reports put up to Senior
children.text:
(v) reviewing any adverse development mentioned in reports put up to Senior
Management on the monitoring and control activities.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Chapter III - Outsourcing of Information Technology (IT) Services
hierarchy.text: Chapter III - Outsourcing of Information Technology (IT) Services

children.title: A. Authorisation, Accountability, and Oversight
children.text:
A. Authorisation, Accountability, and Oversight
11.
As stated in paragraph 8, the outsourcing of any IT service by a bank shall not
diminish its obligations, and those of its Board and Senior Management, who have
the ultimate responsibility for the outsourced service.
12.
A bank shall ensure that:

children.title: (i) the service provider employs the same high standard of care in performing
(ii) it shall not engage a service provider that would result in its reputation being
children.text:
(ii) it shall not engage a service provider that would result in its reputation being
compromised or weakened;

children.title: (iii) notwithstanding whether the service provider is located in India or abroad,
(iv) outsourcing does not impede the RBI in carrying out its supervisory functions
children.text:
(iv) outsourcing does not impede the RBI in carrying out its supervisory functions
and objectives;

children.title: (v) all relevant laws, regulations, rules, guidelines and conditions of approval,
children.text:
(v) all relevant laws, regulations, rules, guidelines and conditions of approval,
licensing or registration have been considered when performing due
diligence in relation to outsourcing; and

children.title: (vi) the service provider, if not a group company, is not owned or controlled by
children.text:
(vi) the service provider, if not a group company, is not owned or controlled by
any director, or key managerial personnel, or approver of the outsourcing
arrangement of the bank, or their relatives. The terms 'control', 'director', 'key
managerial personnel', and 'relative' shall have the same meaning as
assigned under the Companies Act, 2013, and the Rules framed thereunder
from time to time.
Provided that an exception to the above requirement may be made with the
approval of Board or a Committee of the Board, followed by appropriate
disclosure, oversight, and monitoring of such arrangements.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 13. A bank shall evaluate the need for outsourcing of IT services based on a
children.text:
13. A bank shall evaluate the need for outsourcing of IT services based on a
comprehensive assessment of attendant benefits, risks and availability of
commensurate processes to manage those risks. For this purpose, the bank shall,
inter alia, consider the following:

children.title: (i) the need for outsourcing based on materiality / criticality of service to be
children.text:
(i) the need for outsourcing based on materiality / criticality of service to be
outsourced;

children.title: (ii) expectations and outcomes from outsourcing;
children.text: (ii) expectations and outcomes from outsourcing;

children.title: (iii) success factors and cost-benefit analysis; and
children.text: (iii) success factors and cost-benefit analysis; and

children.title: (iv) the model for outsourcing.
children.text: (iv) the model for outsourcing.

children.title: 14. A bank shall ensure that cyber incidents are reported to it by the service provider
children.text:
14. A bank shall ensure that cyber incidents are reported to it by the service provider
without undue delay, so that an incident is reported by the bank to the RBI within
six hours of detection by the service provider.

children.title: B. Governance Framework
children.text:
B. Governance Framework
B.1 Outsourcing Policy

children.title: 15. A bank intending to outsource any of its IT services shall put in place a
children.text:
15. A bank intending to outsource any of its IT services shall put in place a
comprehensive Board-approved IT outsourcing policy incorporating, inter alia, the
following:

children.title: (i) the roles and responsibilities of the Board, Committees of the Board (if any)
(ii) criteria for selection of such services and service providers;
children.text: (ii) criteria for selection of such services and service providers;

children.title: (iii) parameters for defining material outsourcing based on the broad criteria
children.text:
(iii) parameters for defining material outsourcing based on the broad criteria
defined in paragraph 6(3) of these Directions;

children.title: (iv) delegation of authority depending on risk and materiality;
children.text: (iv) delegation of authority depending on risk and materiality;

children.title: (v) disaster recovery and business continuity plans;
children.text: (v) disaster recovery and business continuity plans;

children.title: (vi) systems to monitor and review the operations of these services; and
children.text: (vi) systems to monitor and review the operations of these services; and

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (vii) termination processes and exit strategies, including business continuity in the
children.text:
(vii) termination processes and exit strategies, including business continuity in the
event of a service provider exiting the outsourcing arrangement.
B.2 Role of Senior Management

children.title: 16. The Senior Management of a bank shall, inter alia, be responsible for:
children.text: 16. The Senior Management of a bank shall, inter alia, be responsible for:

children.title: (i) formulating IT outsourcing policies and procedures, evaluating the risks and
children.text:
(i) formulating IT outsourcing policies and procedures, evaluating the risks and
materiality of all existing and prospective IT outsourcing arrangements based
on the framework commensurate with the complexity, nature, and scope, in
line with the enterprise-wide risk management of the bank approved by the
Board and its implementation;

children.title: (ii) prior evaluation of prospective IT outsourcing arrangements and periodic
children.text:
(ii) prior evaluation of prospective IT outsourcing arrangements and periodic
evaluation of the existing IT outsourcing arrangements covering the
performance review, criticality and associated risks of all such arrangements
based on the policy approved by the Board;

children.title: (iii) identifying IT outsourcing risks as they arise, monitoring, mitigating,
(iv) ensuring that suitable business continuity plans based on realistic and
(v) ensuring (a) effective oversight over the service provider (and its
(vi) ensuring an independent review and audit on a periodic basis for compliance
children.text:
(vi) ensuring an independent review and audit on a periodic basis for compliance
with the legislations, regulations, Board-approved policy and performance
standards and reporting the same to Board or a Committee of the Board; and

children.title: (vii) creating essential capacity with required skillsets within the bank for proper
children.text:
(vii) creating essential capacity with required skillsets within the bank for proper
oversight of outsourced services.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
B.3 Role of IT Function

children.title: 17. The responsibilities of the IT Function of a bank shall, inter alia, include:
children.text: 17. The responsibilities of the IT Function of a bank shall, inter alia, include:

children.title: (i) assisting the Senior Management in identifying, measuring, monitoring,
children.text:
(i) assisting the Senior Management in identifying, measuring, monitoring,
mitigating and managing the level of IT outsourcing risk in the bank;

children.title: (ii) ensuring that a central database of all IT outsourcing arrangements is
(iii) effectively monitoring and supervising the outsourced IT service to ensure
children.text:
(iii) effectively monitoring and supervising the outsourced IT service to ensure
that the service providers meet the laid down performance standards and
provide uninterrupted services, reporting to the Senior Management, and co-
ordinating periodic due diligence, and highlighting concerns, if any; and

children.title: (iv) putting in place necessary documentation required for contractual
children.text:
(iv) putting in place necessary documentation required for contractual
agreements including service level management, monitoring of vendor
operations, key risk indicators, and classifying the vendors as per the
determined risk.

children.title: C. Risk Management
children.text:
C. Risk Management
C.1 Risk Management Framework

children.title: 18. A bank shall put in place a risk management framework that comprehensively
children.text:
18. A bank shall put in place a risk management framework that comprehensively
deals with the processes and responsibilities for identification, measurement,
mitigation, management, and reporting of risks associated with such IT
outsourcing arrangements.

children.title: 19. A bank shall suitably document risk assessments with necessary approvals in line
children.text:
19. A bank shall suitably document risk assessments with necessary approvals in line
with the roles and responsibilities of the Board of Directors, Senior Management,
and IT Function, and subject the same to internal and external quality assurance
on a periodic basis as determined by the Board-approved policy.

children.title: 20. A bank shall effectively assess the impact of concentration risk posed by multiple
children.text:
20. A bank shall effectively assess the impact of concentration risk posed by multiple
outsourcing arrangements to the same service provider and the concentration risk

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
posed by outsourcing critical or material functions to a limited number of service
providers.
C.2 Confidentiality and Security of Information

children.title: 21. A bank shall be responsible for the confidentiality and integrity of data and
children.text:
21. A bank shall be responsible for the confidentiality and integrity of data and
information pertaining to its customers that are available to the service provider.

children.title: 22. Access by service providers to data at the bank or its data centre shall be on 'need
children.text:
22. Access by service providers to data at the bank or its data centre shall be on 'need
to know' basis, with appropriate controls to prevent security breaches, or data
misuse.

children.title: 23. A bank shall seek to ensure the security, preservation, and protection of the
children.text:
23. A bank shall seek to ensure the security, preservation, and protection of the
customer information in the custody or possession of the service provider. Access
to customer information by a service provider or its staff shall be on a 'need to
know' basis.

children.title: 24. In the event of multiple service provider relationships where two or more service
children.text:
24. In the event of multiple service provider relationships where two or more service
providers collaborate to deliver an end-to-end solution, the bank remains
responsible for understanding and monitoring the control environment of all service
providers that have access to its data, systems, records, or resources.

children.title: 25. In instances, where a service provider acts as an outsourcing agent for multiple
children.text:
25. In instances, where a service provider acts as an outsourcing agent for multiple
REs, the bank shall take care to build adequate safeguards so that there is no
combining of information, documents, records, and assets.

children.title: Explanation: With regard to requirement that data should not be combined or
children.text:
Explanation: With regard to requirement that data should not be combined or
comingled, it would suffice if there is clear separation and isolation of data (bank,
and its customer specific data and information) to ensure that only the personnel
as authorised by the bank is able to access data that belongs to them in a multi-
tenant environment / architecture.

children.title: 26. A bank shall review and monitor the security practices and control processes of its
children.text:
26. A bank shall review and monitor the security practices and control processes of its
service providers on a regular basis and require the service provider to disclose
security breaches.

children.title: 27. A bank shall immediately notify RBI in the event of breach of security, and leakage
children.text:
27. A bank shall immediately notify RBI in the event of breach of security, and leakage
of confidential customer-related information. In these eventualities, the bank shall

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
adhere to the extant instructions issued by RBI from time to time on Incident
Response and Recovery Management.

children.title: D. Outsourcing Process
children.text:
D. Outsourcing Process
D.1 Service Provider Evaluation
28.
A bank shall perform appropriate due diligence while considering or renewing an
outsourcing arrangement, to assess the capability of the service provider to
comply with obligations in the outsourcing agreement on an ongoing basis.

children.title: 29. The due diligence mentioned above shall involve an evaluation of all available
children.text:
29. The due diligence mentioned above shall involve an evaluation of all available
information, as applicable, about the service provider, including but not limited to:

children.title: (i) qualitative, quantitative, financial, operational, legal, and reputational factors;
children.text: (i) qualitative, quantitative, financial, operational, legal, and reputational factors;

children.title: (ii) while evaluating the capability of the service provider, risks arising from
(iii) past experience and demonstrated competence to implement and support
children.text:
(iii) past experience and demonstrated competence to implement and support
the proposed service over the contracted period;

children.title: (iv) financial soundness and ability to service commitments even under adverse
children.text:
(iv) financial soundness and ability to service commitments even under adverse
conditions;

children.title: (v) business reputation and culture, compliance, complaints and outstanding or
children.text:
(v) business reputation and culture, compliance, complaints and outstanding or
potential litigation;

children.title: (vi) conflict of interest, if any;
children.text: (vi) conflict of interest, if any;

children.title: (vii) external factors like political, economic, social and legal environment of the
(viii) technology, infrastructural stability, security, and internal control, audit
(ix) capability to identify and segregate the bank's data;
children.text: (ix) capability to identify and segregate the bank's data;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (x) quality of due diligence exercised by the service provider of its employees
children.text:
(x) quality of due diligence exercised by the service provider of its employees
and subcontractors;

children.title: (xi) capability to comply with the regulatory, and legal requirements of the
children.text:
(xi) capability to comply with the regulatory, and legal requirements of the
outsourcing arrangement;

children.title: (xii) information / cyber security risk assessment;
children.text: (xii) information / cyber security risk assessment;

children.title: (xiii) ensuring that appropriate controls, assurance requirements, and contractual
(xiv) ability to effectively service all the customers while maintaining confidentiality,
children.text:
(xiv) ability to effectively service all the customers while maintaining confidentiality,
especially where a service provider has exposure to multiple entities; and

children.title: (xv) ability to enforce agreements and the rights available thereunder including
children.text:
(xv) ability to enforce agreements and the rights available thereunder including
those relating to aspects such as data storage, data protection, and
confidentiality

children.title: 30. A bank shall adopt a risk-based approach in conducting such due diligence
children.text:
30. A bank shall adopt a risk-based approach in conducting such due diligence
activities.

children.title: 31. Where possible, a bank shall obtain independent reviews and market feedback on
children.text:
31. Where possible, a bank shall obtain independent reviews and market feedback on
the service provider to supplement the findings of its own due diligence.
D.2 Outsourcing Agreement

children.title: 32. A bank shall ensure that its rights and obligations and those of each service
children.text:
32. A bank shall ensure that its rights and obligations and those of each service
provider are clearly defined and set out in a legally binding written agreement. In
principle, the provisions of the agreement shall appropriately reckon the criticality
of the outsourced task to the business of the bank, the associated risks, and the
strategies for mitigating or managing them.

children.title: 33. The terms and conditions governing the outsourcing arrangement shall be
children.text:
33. The terms and conditions governing the outsourcing arrangement shall be
carefully defined and vetted by the bank's legal counsel on their legal effect and
enforceability. The bank shall ensure that such an agreement is sufficiently flexible
to allow the bank to retain an adequate control over the outsourced service, and
the right to intervene with appropriate measures to meet legal and regulatory

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
obligations. The agreement shall also bring out the nature of legal relationship
between the parties.

children.title: 34. The agreement at a minimum shall include (as applicable to the scope of these
children.text:
34. The agreement at a minimum shall include (as applicable to the scope of these
Directions) the following aspects:

children.title: (i) details of the service being outsourced, including appropriate service and
children.text:
(i) details of the service being outsourced, including appropriate service and
performance standards including for the sub-contractors, if any;

children.title: (ii) effective access by the bank to all data, books, records, information, logs,
(iii) regular monitoring and assessment by the bank of the service provider for
(iv) type of material adverse events (e.g., data breaches, denial of service, and
children.text:
(iv) type of material adverse events (e.g., data breaches, denial of service, and
service unavailability relevant to the outsourced service) and the incidents
required to be reported to the bank, to enable the bank to take prompt risk
mitigation measures, and ensure compliance with statutory and regulatory
guidelines;

children.title: (v) compliance with the provisions of Information Technology Act, 2000, and
(vi) the deliverables including Service Level Agreements (SLAs) formalising
children.text:
(vi) the deliverables including Service Level Agreements (SLAs) formalising
performance criteria to measure the quality and quantity of service levels;

children.title: (vii) storage of data only in India (as applicable) as per extant regulatory
children.text:
(vii) storage of data only in India (as applicable) as per extant regulatory
requirements;

children.title: (viii) clauses requiring the service provider to provide details of data (related to
children.text:
(viii) clauses requiring the service provider to provide details of data (related to
the bank and its customers) captured, processed, and stored;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (ix) controls for maintaining confidentiality of data of the bank and its
(x) types of data / information that the service provider is permitted to share
children.text:
(x) types of data / information that the service provider is permitted to share
with bank's customer or any other party;

children.title: (xi) the resolution process, events of default, indemnities, remedies, and
children.text:
(xi) the resolution process, events of default, indemnities, remedies, and
recourse available to the respective parties;

children.title: (xii) contingency plan(s) to ensure business continuity and testing
children.text:
(xii) contingency plan(s) to ensure business continuity and testing
requirements;

children.title: (xiii) right to conduct audits on the service provider (including its
children.text:
(xiii) right to conduct audits on the service provider (including its
subcontractors) by the bank, whether by its internal or external auditors, or
by agents appointed to act on its behalf, and to obtain copies of any audit
or review reports and findings made on the service provider in conjunction
with the services performed for the bank;

children.title: (xiv) right to seek information from the service provider about the third parties
children.text:
(xiv) right to seek information from the service provider about the third parties
(in the supply chain) engaged by the former;

children.title: (xv) recognising the authority of RBI or person(s) authorised by it to perform
children.text:
(xv) recognising the authority of RBI or person(s) authorised by it to perform
inspection of the service provider and any of its sub-contractors.

children.title: (xvi) clauses to allow RBI or person(s) authorised by it to access the bank's IT
children.text:
(xvi) clauses to allow RBI or person(s) authorised by it to access the bank's IT
infrastructure, applications, data, documents, and other necessary
information given to, stored or processed by the service provider or its
sub-contractors in relation and as applicable to the scope of the
outsourcing arrangement;

children.title: (xvii) clauses making the service provider contractually liable for the
children.text:
(xvii) clauses making the service provider contractually liable for the
performance and risk management practices of its subcontractors;

children.title: (xviii) obligation of the service provider to comply with directions issued by the
<image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (xix) clauses requiring prior approval or consent, as applicable, of the bank for
(xx) termination rights of the bank, including the ability to orderly transfer the
(xxi) obligation of the service provider to co-operate with the relevant authorities
children.text:
(xxi) obligation of the service provider to co-operate with the relevant authorities
in case of insolvency / resolution of the bank;

children.title: (xxii) provision to consider skilled resources of service provider who provide
children.text:
(xxii) provision to consider skilled resources of service provider who provide
core services as 'essential personnel' so that a limited number of staff with
back-up arrangements necessary to operate critical functions can work on-
site during exigencies (including pandemic situations);

children.title: (xxiii) clause requiring suitable back-to-back arrangements between service
children.text:
(xxiii) clause requiring suitable back-to-back arrangements between service
providers and the OEMs; and

children.title: (xxiv) clause requiring non-disclosure agreement with respect to information
children.text:
(xxiv) clause requiring non-disclosure agreement with respect to information
retained by the service provider.
D.3 Monitoring and Control of Outsourced Services

children.title: 35. A bank shall have in place a management structure to monitor and control its
children.text:
35. A bank shall have in place a management structure to monitor and control its
outsourced services. This shall include (as applicable to the scope of these
Directions), but not be limited to, monitoring the performance, uptime of the
systems and resources, service availability, adherence to SLA requirements, and
incident response mechanism.

children.title: 36. A bank shall conduct regular audits (as applicable to the scope of these Directions)
children.text:
36. A bank shall conduct regular audits (as applicable to the scope of these Directions)
of service providers (including sub-contractors) with regard to the service
outsourced by it. Such audits may be conducted either by bank's internal auditors
or external auditors appointed to act on bank's behalf.

children.title: 37. While outsourcing various IT services, more than one RE may be availing services
children.text:
37. While outsourcing various IT services, more than one RE may be availing services
from the same service provider. In such scenarios, in lieu of conducting separate
audits by individual REs of the common service provider, they may adopt pooled

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(shared) audit. This allows the relevant REs to either pool their audit resources or
engage an independent third-party auditor to jointly audit a common service
provider. However, in doing so, it shall be the responsibility of REs in ensuring that
the audit requirements related to their respective contract with the service provider
are met effectively.

children.title: 38. The audits shall, inter alia, assess the performance of the service provider,
children.text:
38. The audits shall, inter alia, assess the performance of the service provider,
adequacy of the risk management practices adopted by the service provider, and
compliance with laws and regulations. The frequency of the audit shall be
determined based on the nature and extent of risk, and impact on the bank from
the outsourcing arrangements. Reports on the monitoring and control activities
shall be reviewed periodically by the Senior Management, and in case of any
adverse development, the same shall be put up to the Board for information.

children.title: 39. A bank, depending upon the risk assessment, may also rely upon globally
children.text:
39. A bank, depending upon the risk assessment, may also rely upon globally
recognised third-party certifications made available by the service provider in lieu
of conducting independent audits. However, this shall not absolve the bank of its
responsibility in ensuring assurance on the controls, and procedures required to
safeguard data security (including availability of systems) at the service provider's
end.

children.title: 40. A bank shall periodically review the financial and operational condition of the
children.text:
40. A bank shall periodically review the financial and operational condition of the
service provider to assess its ability to continue to meet its obligations. A bank
shall adopt risk-based approach in defining the periodicity. Such due diligence
reviews shall highlight any deterioration or breach in performance standards,
confidentiality and security, and operational resilience preparedness.

children.title: 41. A bank shall ensure that the service provider grants unrestricted and effective
children.text:
41. A bank shall ensure that the service provider grants unrestricted and effective
access to (a) data related to the outsourced services; (b) the relevant business
premises of the service provider; subject to appropriate security protocols, for the
purpose of effective oversight by the bank, its auditors, RBI, and other relevant
Competent Authorities, as authorised under law.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
D.4 Inventory of Outsourced Services

children.title: 42. A bank shall create an inventory of IT services outsourced to service providers
children.text:
42. A bank shall create an inventory of IT services outsourced to service providers
(including key entities involved in their supply chains). Further, the bank shall map
its dependency on third parties, and periodically evaluate the information received
from the service providers.
D.5 Business Continuity and Management of Disaster Recovery Plan

children.title: 43. A bank shall require its service providers to develop and establish a robust
children.text:
43. A bank shall require its service providers to develop and establish a robust
framework for documenting, maintaining, and testing Business Continuity Plan
(BCP), and Disaster Recovery Plan (DRP) commensurate with the nature, and
scope of the outsourced service as per extant instructions issued by RBI from time
to time on BCP / DR requirements.
44.
In establishing a viable contingency plan, a bank shall consider the availability of
alternative service providers or the possibility of bringing the outsourced service
back in-house in an emergency, and the costs, time and resources that would be
involved.
45.
In order to mitigate the risk of unexpected termination of the outsourcing
agreement or insolvency or liquidation of its service provider, a bank shall retain
an appropriate level of control over its outsourcing arrangement along with the
right to intervene with appropriate measures to continue its business operations.

children.title: 46. A bank shall ensure that its service providers are able to isolate the bank's
children.text:
46. A bank shall ensure that its service providers are able to isolate the bank's
information, documents, and records, and other assets so that in adverse
conditions or termination of the agreement, all documents, records of transactions,
and information given to the service provider, and assets of the bank, can be
removed from the possession of the service provider, or deleted, destroyed or
rendered unusable.
D.6 Exit Strategy

children.title: 47. The IT outsourcing policy shall contain a clear exit strategy with regard to
children.text:
47. The IT outsourcing policy shall contain a clear exit strategy with regard to
outsourced IT services, for ensuring business continuity during and after exit.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 48. The strategy shall include plans for different scenarios of exit, or termination of
children.text:
48. The strategy shall include plans for different scenarios of exit, or termination of
services with stipulation of minimum period to execute such plans, as necessary.

children.title: 49. In documenting its exit strategy, a bank shall, inter alia, identify alternative
children.text:
49. In documenting its exit strategy, a bank shall, inter alia, identify alternative
arrangements, which may include performing the service by a different service
provider or by the bank itself.

children.title: 50. A service provider shall be legally obliged to co-operate fully with both the bank,
children.text:
50. A service provider shall be legally obliged to co-operate fully with both the bank,
and its new service provider(s) to ensure there is a smooth transition.

children.title: 51. A bank shall ensure that outsourcing agreements have necessary clauses on safe
children.text:
51. A bank shall ensure that outsourcing agreements have necessary clauses on safe
removal or destruction of data, hardware and all records (digital and physical), as
applicable.

children.title: 52. The outsourcing agreement shall ensure that the service provider is prohibited
children.text:
52. The outsourcing agreement shall ensure that the service provider is prohibited
from erasing, purging, revoking, altering or changing any data during the transition
period, unless specifically advised by the RBI or the concerned bank.
D.7 Termination

children.title: 53. In the event of termination of the outsourcing agreement for any reason in cases
children.text:
53. In the event of termination of the outsourcing agreement for any reason in cases
where the service provider deals with the customers of the bank, the same shall be
given due publicity by the bank so as to ensure that the customers stop dealing
with the concerned service provider.

children.title: E. Specific Outsourcing Arrangements
children.text:
E. Specific Outsourcing Arrangements
E.1 Outsourcing within a Group / Conglomerate

children.title: 54. A bank may outsource any IT service within its business group / conglomerate,
children.text:
54. A bank may outsource any IT service within its business group / conglomerate,
provided that such an arrangement is backed by the Board-approved policy, and
appropriate SLAs / agreements with its group entities are in place.

children.title: 55. The selection of a group entity shall be based on objective reasons that are similar
children.text:
55. The selection of a group entity shall be based on objective reasons that are similar
to selection of a third-party, and the bank shall appropriately deal with any conflicts
of interest that such an outsourcing arrangement may entail.

children.title: 56. A bank, at all times, shall maintain an arm's length relationship in dealings with its
children.text:
56. A bank, at all times, shall maintain an arm's length relationship in dealings with its
group entities. Risk management practices being adopted by the bank while

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
outsourcing to a group entity shall be identical to those specified for a non-related
party.
E.2 Offshore or Cross-Border Outsourcing

children.title: 57. In principle, outsourcing arrangements shall only be entered into with parties
children.text:
57. In principle, outsourcing arrangements shall only be entered into with parties
operating in jurisdictions that generally uphold confidentiality clauses, and
agreements.

children.title: 58. While engaging with service provider(s) in a foreign country, a bank shall:
children.text: 58. While engaging with service provider(s) in a foreign country, a bank shall:

children.title: (i) closely monitor government policies of the jurisdiction in which the service
children.text:
(i) closely monitor government policies of the jurisdiction in which the service
provider is based and the political, social, economic, and legal conditions on
a continuous basis, and establish sound procedures for mitigating the country
risk. This includes, inter alia, having appropriate contingency and exit
strategies;

children.title: (ii) clearly specify the governing law of the outsourcing arrangement;
children.text: (ii) clearly specify the governing law of the outsourcing arrangement;

children.title: (iii) ensure that availability of records to the bank, and the RBI will not be affected
children.text:
(iii) ensure that availability of records to the bank, and the RBI will not be affected
even in case of liquidation of the service provider;

children.title: (iv) ensure the right of the bank, and RBI to direct, and conduct audit or
children.text:
(iv) ensure the right of the bank, and RBI to direct, and conduct audit or
inspection of the service provider based in a foreign jurisdiction; and

children.title: (v) ensure that the arrangement complies with all statutory requirements as well
children.text:
(v) ensure that the arrangement complies with all statutory requirements as well
as regulations issued by the RBI from time to time.
E.3 Outsourcing of Security Operations Centre (SOC)

children.title: 59. Considering the risks associated with outsourcing of Security Operations Centre
children.text:
59. Considering the risks associated with outsourcing of Security Operations Centre
(SOC) operations by a bank, such as data being stored and processed at an
external location and managed by the service provider (or its sub-contractors)to
which the bank has lesser visibility, the bank, to mitigate the risks, shall adopt the
following requirements in the case of outsourcing of SOC operations in addition to
the controls prescribed in these Directions:

children.title: (i) unambiguously identify the owner of assets used in providing the services
children.text:
(i) unambiguously identify the owner of assets used in providing the services
(e.g., systems, software, source code, processes, and concepts);

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (ii) ensure that the bank has adequate oversight, and ownership over the rule
children.text: (ii) ensure that the bank has adequate oversight, and ownership over the rule

children.title: definition, customisation, and related data / logs, meta-data, and analytics
children.text:
definition, customisation, and related data / logs, meta-data, and analytics
(specific to the bank);

children.title: (iii) assess SOC functioning, including all physical facilities involved in service
(iv) integrate the outsourced SOC reporting, and escalation process with the
children.text:
(iv) integrate the outsourced SOC reporting, and escalation process with the
bank's incident response process; and

children.title: (v) review the process of handling of the alerts or events.
children.text:
(v) review the process of handling of the alerts or events.
E.4 Usage of Cloud Computing Services

children.title: 60. Several cloud deployment and service models have emerged over time. These are
children.text:
60. Several cloud deployment and service models have emerged over time. These are
generally based on the extent of technology stack that is proposed to be adopted
by the consuming entity.
(1) Example - 1: (i) Some cloud services are:

children.title: (a) Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS): The service provides computing,
children.text:
(a) Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS): The service provides computing,
storage, network, and other basic resources so that the client can
develop and deploy their applications.

children.title: (b) Platform as a Service (PaaS): The service provides software for
children.text:
(b) Platform as a Service (PaaS): The service provides software for
building application, middleware, database, development environment,
and other tools along with the infrastructure to the client.

children.title: (c) Software as a Service (SaaS): Client uses the application(s) provided
children.text:
(c) Software as a Service (SaaS): Client uses the application(s) provided
by the service provider on a cloud infrastructure.

children.title: (ii) Besides the three common application services, Cloud Service
children.text:
(ii) Besides the three common application services, Cloud Service
Providers (CSPs) also provide a range of services, viz., Database as a
Service, Security as a Service, Storage as a Service, and others with
varying risk levels.
(2) Example - 2: Some of the popular deployment models for delivery of cloud
services are Private Cloud, Public Cloud, Hybrid Cloud, and Community Cloud.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 61. Considering the varied services, benefits, and risk profiles associated with the
children.text:
61. Considering the varied services, benefits, and risk profiles associated with the
cloud deployment and service models, a bank that uses cloud services for storage,
computing and movement of data in cloud environments shall, in addition to other
applicable provisions in these Directions:

children.title: (i) undertake a comprehensive assessment of its business strategy, and goals
children.text:
(i) undertake a comprehensive assessment of its business strategy, and goals
adopted to the existing IT applications' footprint, and associated costs. Such
assessment shall include, but not be limited to, an analysis of various heads
of cloud-related expenditure, such as application refactoring, integration,
consulting, migration, and projected recurring expenditure depending on the
nature of workloads. The extent of cloud adoption may vary, ranging from
migration of non-business critical workloads to the cloud, to deployment of
critical business applications such as SaaS, or other combinations in
between, and shall be determined based on a duly conducted business
technology risk assessment;

children.title: (ii) ensure, inter alia, that the 'IT outsourcing policy', referred to in paragraph 15
children.text:
(ii) ensure, inter alia, that the 'IT outsourcing policy', referred to in paragraph 15
of these Directions, addresses the entire lifecycle of data, i.e., covering the
entire span of time from generation of the data to its entry into the cloud, and
till the data is permanently erased or deleted. It shall also ensure that
specified procedures are consistent with business needs and legal and
regulatory requirements;

children.title: (iii) take into account cloud service specific factors, viz., multi-tenancy, and multi-
(iv) implement necessary controls by referring to the cloud security best
children.text:
(iv) implement necessary controls by referring to the cloud security best
practices, as per applicability of the shared responsibility model between the
bank and the Cloud Service Provider (CSP);
For cloud security best practices, a bank may refer to, inter alia, NIST SP
800-210 General Access Control Guidance for Cloud Systems
https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/SpecialPublications/NIST.SP.800-210.pdf

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (v) put in place strong cloud governance by adopting and demonstrating a well-
children.text:
(v) put in place strong cloud governance by adopting and demonstrating a well-
established and documented cloud adoption policy. Such a policy shall, inter
alia,

children.title: (a) identify the services that can be moved to the cloud;
children.text: (a) identify the services that can be moved to the cloud;

children.title: (b) enable and support protection of various stakeholder interests;
children.text: (b) enable and support protection of various stakeholder interests;

children.title: (c) ensure compliance with regulatory requirements, including those on
children.text:
(c) ensure compliance with regulatory requirements, including those on
privacy, security, data sovereignty, recoverability, and data storage
requirements, aligned with data classification; and

children.title: (d) provide for appropriate due diligence to manage and continually
children.text:
(d) provide for appropriate due diligence to manage and continually
monitor the risks associated with CSPs;

children.title: (vi) ensure that the selection of a CSP is based on a comprehensive risk
children.text:
(vi) ensure that the selection of a CSP is based on a comprehensive risk
assessment of the CSP. A bank shall enter into a contract only with CSPs
that are subject to jurisdictions that uphold enforceability of agreements, and
the rights available thereunder to the bank, including those relating to
aspects such as data storage, data protection, and confidentiality;

children.title: (vii) ensure that the service and technology architecture supporting cloud-based
children.text:
(vii) ensure that the service and technology architecture supporting cloud-based
applications is built in adherence to globally recognised architecture
principles, and standards. The technology architecture shall:

children.title: (a) provide for a standard set of tools and processes to manage
children.text:
(a) provide for a standard set of tools and processes to manage
containers, images, and releases;

children.title: (b) provide for a secure container-based data management, where
(c) be protected against data integrity and confidentiality risks, and
<image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (d) be resilient, and enable smooth recovery in case of failure of any one
(viii) agree upon the Identity and Access Management (IAM) with the CSP and
children.text:
(viii) agree upon the Identity and Access Management (IAM) with the CSP and
ensure that role-based access to the cloud hosted applications, both in
respect of user-access and privileged-access, is provided. The bank shall:

children.title: (a) establish stringent access controls, as applicable for an on-premises
children.text:
(a) establish stringent access controls, as applicable for an on-premises
application, for IAM to cloud-based applications;

children.title: (b) implement segregation of duties, role conflict matrix for all kinds of
(c) ensure that access provisioning is governed by principles of 'need to
children.text:
(c) ensure that access provisioning is governed by principles of 'need to
know', and 'least privileges'; and

children.title: (d) implement multi-factor authentication for access to cloud
children.text:
(d) implement multi-factor authentication for access to cloud
applications.

children.title: (ix) ensure that the implementation of security controls in the cloud-based
children.text:
(ix) ensure that the implementation of security controls in the cloud-based
application achieves similar or higher degree of control objectives than those
achieved in or by an on-premises application. This includes ensuring secure
connection through appropriate deployment of network security resources
and their configurations; appropriate and secure configurations, monitoring of
the cloud assets utilised by the bank, and necessary procedures to authorise
changes to cloud applications, and related resources.

children.title: (x) define minimum monitoring requirements in the cloud environment and
children.text:
(x) define minimum monitoring requirements in the cloud environment and
assess the information / cyber security capability of the CSP, to ensure that
it:

children.title: (a) maintains an information security policy framework commensurate
children.text:
(a) maintains an information security policy framework commensurate
with its exposures to vulnerabilities and threats;

children.title: (b) is able to maintain its information / cyber security capability with
children.text:
(b) is able to maintain its information / cyber security capability with
respect to changes in vulnerabilities and threats, including those

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
resulting from changes to information assets or its business
environment;

children.title: (c) has set the nature and frequency of testing of controls in respect of
children.text:
(c) has set the nature and frequency of testing of controls in respect of
the outsourced services commensurate with the materiality of the
services being outsourced by the bank, and the threat environment;
and

children.title: (d) has mechanisms in place to assess the subcontractors with regards
(xi) ensure appropriate integration of logs and events from the CSP into the
children.text:
(xi) ensure appropriate integration of logs and events from the CSP into the
bank's SOC, wherever applicable, and retention of relevant logs in cloud for
incident reporting, and handling of incidents relating to services deployed on
the cloud;

children.title: (xii) ensure that the cyber resilience controls of the CSP complement the bank's
children.text:
(xii) ensure that the cyber resilience controls of the CSP complement the bank's
own application security measures, and that both the bank and the CSP
maintain continuous and regular updates of security-related software,
including upgrades, fixes, patches, and service packs, to safeguard
applications against advanced threats and malware;

children.title: (xiii) ensure that the CSP has a well-governed and structured approach to
(xiv) ensure that the business continuity framework provides for continued
children.text:
(xiv) ensure that the business continuity framework provides for continued
operation of critical functions in the event of a disaster affecting the bank's
cloud services or failure of the CSP, with minimal disruption to services, and
without compromising data integrity and security;

children.title: (xv) ensure that the CSP has put in place demonstrative capabilities for
children.text:
(xv) ensure that the CSP has put in place demonstrative capabilities for
preparedness and readiness for cyber resilience as regards cloud services in
use by them through, inter alia, robust incident response and recovery

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
practices including conduct of DR drills at various levels of cloud services
including necessary stakeholders;

children.title: (xvi) develop an exit strategy that shall
children.text: (xvi) develop an exit strategy that shall

children.title: (a) factor, inter alia, agreed processes and turnaround times for
children.text:
(a) factor, inter alia, agreed processes and turnaround times for
returning the bank's service collaterals and data held by the CSP,
data completeness, and portability, secure purge of bank's
information from the CSP's environment, smooth transition of
services, and unambiguous definition of liabilities, damages,
penalties and indemnities, which should also be a part of the service
level stipulations in SLA;

children.title: (b) include exit plans which align with the ongoing design of applications
children.text:
(b) include exit plans which align with the ongoing design of applications
and service delivery technology stack;

children.title: (c) include contractually agreed exit / termination plans, which specify
children.text:
(c) include contractually agreed exit / termination plans, which specify
how the cloud-hosted service(s) and data will be moved out from the
cloud with minimal impact on continuity of the bank's business, while
maintaining integrity and security; and

children.title: (d) include clauses for prompt take-over of all records of transactions,
children.text:
(d) include clauses for prompt take-over of all records of transactions,
customer, and operational information, and configuration data in a
systematic manner from the CSP, and purging at the CSP end, and
ensuring independent assurance before signing off from the CSP;

children.title: (xvii) ensure that the audit / periodic review / third-party certifications cover, as per
children.text:
(xvii) ensure that the audit / periodic review / third-party certifications cover, as per
applicability and cloud usage, inter alia, aspects such as roles and
responsibilities of both bank and CSP in cloud governance, access and
network controls, configurations, monitoring mechanism, data encryption, log
review, change management, incident response, and resilience
preparedness and testing.

children.title: F. Redressal of Grievances related to Outsourced Services
children.text: F. Redressal of Grievances related to Outsourced Services

children.title: 62. A bank shall have a robust grievance redressal mechanism that shall not be
children.text:
62. A bank shall have a robust grievance redressal mechanism that shall not be
compromised in any manner on account of outsourcing, i.e., responsibility for

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
redressal of customers' grievances related to outsourced services shall rest with
the bank.

children.title: 63. Outsourcing arrangements entered into by a bank shall not affect the rights of its
children.text:
63. Outsourcing arrangements entered into by a bank shall not affect the rights of its
customers against the bank, including the ability of the customers to obtain
redressal as applicable under relevant laws.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Chapter IV - Repeal and Other Provisions
hierarchy.text: Chapter IV - Repeal and Other Provisions

children.title: A. Repeal and Saving
children.text: A. Repeal and Saving

children.title: 64. With the issue of these Directions, the existing Directions, instructions, and
children.text:
64. With the issue of these Directions, the existing Directions, instructions, and
guidelines relating to outsourcing of IT services as applicable to LABs stand
repealed, as communicated vide circular DOR.RRC.REC.302/33-01-010/2025-26
dated November 28, 2025. The Directions, instructions, and guidelines repealed
prior to the issuance of these Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

children.title: 65. Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
children.text:
65. Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken, or
initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to
be governed by the provisions thereof. All approvals or acknowledgments granted
under these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these
Directions. Further, the repeal of these Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
not in any way prejudicially affect:
(1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder;
(2) any, penalty, forfeiture, or punishment incurred in respect of any contravention
committed thereunder;
(3) any investigation, legal proceeding, or remedy in respect of any such right,
privilege, obligation, liability, penalty, forfeiture, or punishment as aforesaid;
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those Directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.

children.title: B. Application of other laws not barred
children.text: B. Application of other laws not barred

children.title: 66. The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
children.text:
66. The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or Directions, for the time being
in force.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: C. Interpretations
children.text: C. Interpretations

children.title: 67. For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions, or in order to
children.text:
67. For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions, or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary clarifications in
respect of any matter covered herein, and the interpretation of any provision of
these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
(Sunil T S Nair) Chief General Manager
